[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (two major seats)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |例祭={{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} / {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{jalink|Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{jalink|Isonokami Jingū|Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{jalink|Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731}}. The name {{jalink|Futsunomitama|Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{jalink|Kunitsukami|Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{jalink|sanukite|Q843420}} used as raw material for {{jalink|stone tools|Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}} under {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{jalink|Katsushimo District|Q11620862}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{jalink|Taima-dera|Q478979}} {{jalink|Nakanobō}} records that there was also a shrine to {{jalink|Shinja Daiō}} ({{jalink|Dragon King|Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{jalink|Gongen|Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{jalink|Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081}}. At {{jalink|Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (two major seats)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |例祭={{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} / {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{jalink|Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{jalink|Isonokami Jingū|Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{jalink|Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731}}. The name {{jalink|Futsunomitama|Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{jalink|Kunitsukami|Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{jalink|sanukite|Q843420}} used as raw material for {{jalink|stone tools|Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}} under {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{jalink|Katsushimo District|Q11620862}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{jalink|Taima-dera|Q478979}} {{jalink|Nakanobō}} records that there was also a shrine to {{jalink|Shinja Daiō}} ({{jalink|Dragon King|Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{jalink|Gongen|Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{jalink|Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081}}. At {{jalink|Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (two major seats)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |例祭={{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} / {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{jalink|Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{jalink|Isonokami Jingū|Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{jalink|Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731}}. The name {{jalink|Futsunomitama|Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{jalink|Kunitsukami|Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{jalink|sanukite|Q843420}} used as raw material for {{jalink|stone tools|Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}} under {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{jalink|Katsushimo District|Q11620862}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{jalink|Taima-dera|Q478979}} {{jalink|Nakanobō}} records that there was also a shrine to {{jalink|Shinja Daiō}} ({{jalink|Dragon King|Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{jalink|Gongen|Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{jalink|Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081}}. At {{jalink|Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (two major seats)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |本殿={{jalink|Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |例祭={{jalink|July 25|Q2720}} / {{jalink|October 25|Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{jalink|Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{jalink|Katsuragi|Q659672}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (major shrine), and under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} its former rank was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{jalink|Kunitama|Q9692242|Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{jalink|Isonokami Jingū|Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{jalink|Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731}}. The name {{jalink|Futsunomitama|Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{jalink|Kunitsukami|Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{jalink|sanukite|Q843420}} used as raw material for {{jalink|stone tools|Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}} under {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} 1 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{jalink|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125}}. In the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{jalink|Katsushimo District|Q11620862}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{jalink|Taima-dera|Q478979}} {{jalink|Nakanobō}} records that there was also a shrine to {{jalink|Shinja Daiō}} ({{jalink|Dragon King|Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{jalink|Gongen|Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{jalink|Shōwa|Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{jalink|Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081}}. At {{jalink|Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979}} {{ill||lt=Nakanobō|UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill||lt=Shinja Daiō|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081}}. At {{ill||lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

[[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

{{ill|Q31510586|ltq=Q31510586|WD=Q31510586}} == public transport stop (P8453) == * {{ill|Q7034581|ltq=Q7034581|WD=Q7034581}} {{commons category|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} [[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

category|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} [[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523|1=Kunitsukami}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420|1=Sanukitoid}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257|1=Stone tool}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862|1=Katsuge district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979|1=Taima-dera}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914|1=Dragon King}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343|1=Gongen}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081|1=Prince Ōtsu}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581|1=Nijōjinjaguchi Station}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

category|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} [[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523|1=Kunitsukami}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420|1=Sanukitoid}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257|1=Stone tool}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862|1=Katsuge district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979|1=Taima-dera}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914|1=Dragon King}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343|1=Gongen}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081|1=Prince Ōtsu}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581|1=Nijōjinjaguchi Station}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523|1=Kunitsukami}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420|1=Sanukitoid}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257|1=Stone tool}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862|1=Katsuge district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979|1=Taima-dera}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914|1=Dragon King}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343|1=Gongen}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081|1=Prince Ōtsu}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581|1=Nijōjinjaguchi Station}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

category|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} [[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}
{{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|WD=Q2720|1=July 25}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|WD=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|WD=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|WD=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|WD=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|WD=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|WD=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|WD=Q11059754|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|WD=Q60995523|1=Kunitsukami}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|WD=Q843420|1=Sanukitoid}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|WD=Q479257|1=Stone tool}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|WD=Q11620862|1=Katsuge district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|WD=Q478979|1=Taima-dera}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|WD=Q135337914|1=Dragon King}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|WD=Q3080343|1=Gongen}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|WD=Q1284081|1=Prince Ōtsu}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|WD=Q7034581|1=Nijōjinjaguchi Station}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かつらきふたかみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧郷社]] [[Category:式内大社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大かつらきふたかみしんしや]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:葛城市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]
