category|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} [[JA:葛木二上神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11620952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Katsuragi Futakami Shrine |画像=[[File:Katsuragi-futakami-jinja, haisho.jpg|280px]]
Worship place
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |所在地={{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|qid=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} Someno 691 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 31 | 緯度秒 = 33 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 40 | 経度秒 = 39 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神={{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|qid=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}
{{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (two major seats)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |本殿={{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=July 25|Q2720|qid=Q2720|1=July 25}} / {{ill|lt=October 25|Q2954|qid=Q2954|1=October 25}} |地図=Japan Nara }} '''Katsuragi Futakami Shrine''' (かつらぎふたかみじんじゃ / かつらぎにじょうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} near the summit of the male peak of {{ill|lt=Mount Nijō (Nara/Osaka)|Q11371271|qid=Q11371271|lt=Mount Nijō|1=Mount Nijō}} in Someno, {{ill|lt=Katsuragi|Q659672|qid=Q659672|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (major shrine), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called “Nijō Shrine” and “Katsuragi ni imasu Futakami Shrine.” === Enshrined deities === It enshrines Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Kunitama|Q9692242|qid=Q9692242|lt=Ōkunitama-no-Kami|1=Kunitama}}. There is a tradition that Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami was invited to {{ill|lt=Isonokami Jingū|Q708859|qid=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}} and Ōkunitama-no-Kami to {{ill|lt=Ōyamato Shrine|Q245731|qid=Q245731|1=Ōyamato Shrine}}. The name {{ill|lt=Futsunomitama|Q11059754|qid=Q11059754|1=Futsu-no-mitama}} is well known for Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, but why “Toyo” is prefixed is unclear. He is also considered the same deity as {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}. Ōkunitama-no-Kami is regarded as the great commander among the {{ill|lt=Kunitsukami|Q60995523|qid=Q60995523|1=Kunitsukami}}. ''Jinja Yōroku'' records “Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto, Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto,” while ''Jingi-shi'' records “Ki Toyofutsu-no-Mitama-no-Kami, Ōkuni-no-Kami.” Originally, male and female deities are thought to have been enshrined separately on the two summits of Mount Nijō (male peak and female peak). === History === The date and circumstances of foundation are unknown. Mount Nijō has layers of {{ill|lt=sanukite|Q843420|qid=Q843420|1=Sanukitoid}} used as raw material for {{ill|lt=stone tools|Q479257|qid=Q479257|1=Stone tool}}, and people are thought to have lived nearby since ancient times. The first documentary appearance is in the {{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), first month, day 27, where the shrine is granted the divine rank of {{ill|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Katsuragi Futakami Shrine, two seats, {{ill|lt=Katsushimo District|Q11620862|qid=Q11620862|1=Katsuge district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}},” and ranked as a major shrine. The “Map of the Vicinity of Taima-dera” held by {{ill|lt=Taima-dera|Q478979|qid=Q478979|1=Taima-dera}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakanobō|1=UNKNOWN}} records that there was also a shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinja Daiō|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Dragon King|Q135337914|qid=Q135337914|1=Dragon King}}) on the female peak of Mount Nijō; tradition says it was moved and is now a small Ryūō shrine under the Inari subsidiary shrine within Nakanobō’s precincts. Its relation to this shrine is unknown. In the early modern period this shrine was called “Take no {{ill|lt=Gongen|Q3080343|qid=Q3080343|1=Gongen}},” and was the ujigami for several dozen villages in the Take-go region using water flow from Mount Nijō. It was ranked as gōsha in April 1873 (Meiji 6). === Precincts === The current shrine building burned in the 1974 ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49) major fire on Mount Nijō and was rebuilt in 1975; inside is a small sacred sakaki object. East of the main shrine is the tomb of {{ill|lt=Prince Ōtsu|Q1284081|qid=Q1284081|1=Prince Ōtsu}}. At {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi Shitatori ni imasu Amenohazuchi-no-Mikoto Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Shitatori Shrine) at the mountain foot, Ōkunitama-no-Kami of this shrine is enshrined as an associated deity. === Festivals === In drought years, villagers of Take-go would climb the mountain for rain prayer chanting “The god of the mountain loves banners, bring banners and let rain fall.” Today, volunteer “Take-nobori” climbs are held annually on April 23. === Other === Entry to the precinct (male peak summit) requires paying a 200-yen environmental preservation cooperation fee to the shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Nijōjinjaguchi Station|Q7034581|qid=Q7034581|1=Nijōjinjaguchi Station}} (にじょうじんじゃぐち) {{commons|Category:Katsuragi-niimasu-futakamino-jinja}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Katsuragi Futakami Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:葛城市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Katsuragi, Nara]]
